title: linum dehiscens subsp. crepitans
intro: Linum dehiscens subsp. crepitans, also known as wild flax or prairie flax, is a subspecies of the flax plant that is native to North America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its high content of mucilage, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects. The health benefits of Linum dehiscens subsp. crepitans include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and salves, can be made by drying the leaves and flowers, which can then be used to make teas, tinctures, or topical applications.
